// BenchmarkRunner.cs
using System;
using System.Collections.Concurrent;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

public sealed class BenchmarkRunner : IDisposable
{
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _resourceLock = new(1, 1); // Enforces single-model concurrency
    private readonly ConcurrentQueue<BenchmarkContext> _queue = new();
    private readonly ConcurrentDictionary<string, BenchmarkResult> _results = new();
    private readonly CancellationTokenSource _cts = new();
    private bool _disposed;

    public void Enqueue(BenchmarkContext context)
    {
        if (_disposed) throw new ObjectDisposedException(nameof(BenchmarkRunner));
        _queue.Enqueue(context);
    }

    public async Task RunAsync()
    {
        while (!_cts.Token.IsCancellationRequested)
        {
            if (_queue.TryDequeue(out var context))
            {
                await _resourceLock.WaitAsync(_cts.Token);
                try
                {
                    var result = await ExecuteModelAsync(context, _cts.Token);
                    _results.TryAdd(context.Model, result);
                }
                finally
                {
                    _resourceLock.Release();
                }
            }
            else if (_queue.IsEmpty)
            {
                break; // Queue empty and no new items
            }
            else
            {
                await Task.Delay(100, _cts.Token); // Backoff if queue temporarily empty
            }
        }
    }

    private async Task<BenchmarkResult> ExecuteModelAsync(BenchmarkContext ctx, CancellationToken token)
    {
        // Mock Docker execution logic
        await Task.Delay(5000, token); // Simulate heavy model load
        return new BenchmarkResult(ctx.Model, ctx
